skill.name: video
skill.description: When the user wants to create, generate, or produce video content using AI tools or programmatic frameworks. Also use when the user mentions 'video production,' 'AI video,' 'Remotion,' 'Hyperframes,' 'HeyGen,' 'Synthesia,' 'Veo,' 'Sora,' 'Runway,' 'Kling,' 'Seedance,' 'Hailuo,' 'MiniMax,' 'Pika,' 'Hunyuan,' 'Wan,' 'video generation,' 'AI avatar,' 'talking head video,' 'programmatic video,' 'video template,' 'explainer video,' 'product demo video,' 'video pipeline,' or 'make me a video.' Use this for video creation, generation, and production workflows. For video content strategy and what to post, see social. For paid video ad creative, see ad-creative.
skill.summary: Create professional AI-generated and programmatic marketing videos with tool recommendations, prompting guides, and production workflows.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 56 potential patterns, but evaluation confirms all are false positives. The skill is a legitimate marketing documentation resource about video production tools (Veo, Runway, HeyGen, Remotion, Hyperframes). 'Weak crypto' flags match text discussing video resolutions and generation counts, not cryptographic code. 'Shell backtick' flags are documentation showing standard npm/npx commands. URLs are links to official tool docs. No malicious intent or executable code found.

low_findings.title: External command examples in documentation
low_findings.description: Skill contains npm/npx installation commands (e.g., `npm install hyperframes`, `npx create-video@latest`). These are documentation examples for users to run themselves, not embedded execution. Low risk as no code runs without user invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matches shell commands but these are standard documentation examples showing users how to install video tools, not embedded executable code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Skill contains URLs to official tool documentation (heygen.com, remotion.dev, runwayml.com). These are reference links for users, not data exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to official vendor documentation sites for the tools being recommended. No credential exfiltration or malicious destinations.

content.user_title: Create Marketing Videos with AI Tools
content.value_statement: Creating marketing videos requires choosing between dozens of AI tools, frameworks, and workflows. This skill guides you through selecting the right approach—whether programmatic, AI-generated, or avatar-based—to produce professional video content efficiently.
actual_capabilities: Recommends the right video production approach based on use case (programmatic, AI generation, AI avatars, or editing/repurposing)
Provides detailed comparisons of AI video models including Veo 3, Sora 2, Runway Gen-4, Kling, and Pika
Guides implementation of programmatic video frameworks like Hyperframes and Remotion with code examples
Explains AI avatar workflows with HeyGen MCP server integration for agent-driven video generation
Offers structured prompting formulas for AI video generation models (Subject + Action + Camera + Style + Mood)
Describes complete production workflows for product demos, explainers, and batch social clips
limitations: Does not execute video generation itself—provides guidance for users to use external tools
Cannot access or control AI video generation APIs directly without user-provided credentials
Video model pricing and availability change frequently; recommendations may need verification
Does not include actual video rendering capabilities—focuses on planning and orchestration

use_cases.title: Product Demo Video Production
use_cases.description: Plan and execute a 2-minute product demo video combining screen recording, programmatic overlays, and AI B-roll footage.
use_cases.target_user: SaaS marketing teams producing homepage demo videos

use_cases.title: Recurring AI Avatar Content
use_cases.description: Set up a weekly video pipeline using HeyGen avatars for product updates without appearing on camera.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders and product teams needing consistent video content

use_cases.title: Batch Personalized Sales Videos
use_cases.description: Generate 50+ personalized intro videos for sales outreach using template-based avatar generation.
use_cases.target_user: Sales teams running personalized outreach campaigns

prompt_templates.title: Video Production Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a [DURATION] video for [PLATFORM]. The goal is [GOAL]. Should I use programmatic video, AI generation, or AI avatars? What tools do you recommend?
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new video project and need to choose the right approach

prompt_templates.title: AI Video Prompt Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write an AI video generation prompt for [SCENE DESCRIPTION]. Include subject, action, camera movement, visual style, and lighting. Optimize for [MODEL: Veo/Runway/Kling/Pika].
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating prompts for AI video generation models

prompt_templates.title: Programmatic Video Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Hyperframes template for [VIDEO TYPE] with [NUMBER] frames. Each frame should display [CONTENT] and last [DURATION] seconds. Output as MP4 at [RESOLUTION].
prompt_templates.scenario: Building templated, data-driven videos with code

prompt_templates.title: Video Repurposing Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a [SOURCE CONTENT: webinar/podcast/demo] of [DURATION]. Help me create a repurposing workflow to extract [NUMBER] short clips for [PLATFORMS]. Which tools should I use for each step?
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting long-form content into multiple short-form social clips

output_examples.input: We need a 2-minute product demo video for our SaaS homepage. What's the fastest way to produce it?
output: Recommended workflow: Product Demo Video pipeline
Step 1: Script the key features and value props
Step 2: Screen record the product flow (don't use AI for UI)
Step 3: Programmatic overlay with Hyperframes for titles/callouts
Step 4: Optional AI B-roll with Veo/Runway for establishing shots
Step 5: Voiceover via recording or HeyGen avatar
Key reminder: Add captions—85% of video is watched without sound

output_examples.input: I want to generate a 10-second clip of a person typing on a laptop in a coffee shop. Which AI tool?
output: Tool recommendation: Veo 3 (highest quality) or Runway Gen-4 (motion control)
Alternative: Kling 3.0 for lower cost at volume

Sample prompt: 'A close-up shot of hands typing on a laptop keyboard in a cozy coffee shop, shallow depth of field, warm afternoon lighting through a window, camera holds steady, cinematic color grading, 4K.'
Pattern: Subject + Action + Camera + Style + Mood

best_practices: Check for product marketing context (.agents/product-marketing.md) before starting to leverage existing brand information
Start with strategy, not tools—decide what type of video you need before selecting generation methods
Use real screen recording for product UI rather than AI generation—models hallucinate interfaces
Always include captions—85% of social video is watched without sound
Generate 3-4 variations of AI video prompts and iterate on the best results
anti_patterns: Starting with tool selection instead of defining video goals and target platform requirements
Using AI generation for product UI screens—results in hallucinated, inaccurate interfaces
Requesting readable text in AI-generated videos—models struggle with typography
Over-producing content when authentic, simple footage would perform better on social platforms

faq.question: What video production approach should I use?
faq.answer: Use programmatic video (Hyperframes/Remotion) for templated, data-driven content. Use AI generation (Veo, Runway, Kling) for original B-roll footage. Use AI avatars (HeyGen, Synthesia) for talking-head presentations without filming. Use editing tools (Descript, Opus Clip) to repurpose existing long-form content.

faq.question: Which AI video generation model is best?
faq.answer: Veo 3 and Sora 2 offer the highest quality with synced audio. Runway Gen-4 provides strong motion control and temporal consistency. Kling offers longer generation at lower cost. Choose based on quality needs, budget, and required duration.

faq.question: Can agents generate videos automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, through MCP server integrations. HeyGen offers an official MCP server for direct avatar video generation. Hyperframes uses plain HTML that any coding agent can produce. Video model APIs accept standard HTTP requests for B-roll generation.

faq.question: How do I write effective AI video prompts?
faq.answer: Follow the structure: Subject + Action + Camera movement + Visual style + Lighting/mood + Technical specs. Be specific about camera movement (pan, dolly, orbit). Always specify style (cinematic, documentary, commercial). Keep prompts under 100 words for best results.

faq.question: What aspect ratio should I use?
faq.answer: Use 16:9 (1920x1080) for YouTube, websites, and LinkedIn. Use 9:16 (1080x1920) for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts. Use 1:1 or 4:5 for Instagram Feed posts. Always specify the aspect ratio in your AI video generation settings.

faq.question: How do I repurpose long videos into short clips?
faq.answer: Use a three-step workflow: Descript cleans up audio and removes filler, Opus Clip auto-extracts the best moments with virality scoring, and CapCut adds captions and platform-specific styling. This converts webinars and podcasts into multiple social-ready clips efficiently.
